channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins as aid groups push to scale up deliveries and US troops land in Israel
item.description: Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians are returning to their neighborhoods as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas enters its second day.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana, Sam Metz, Sarah El Deeb And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kim Jong Un shows off 'most powerful' ballistic missile as foreign leaders watch North Korea military parade
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un unveiled the untested Hwasong-20 ICBM at a Worker's Party 80th anniversary parade, Friday, calling it the most powerful nuclear strategic weapons system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago leaders, residents share mixed emotions and cautious hope after Israel-Hamas ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has showcased his military's most powerful weapons at a massive parade attended by foreign leaders.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung And Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday for her struggle to achieve a democratic transition in the South American nation, winning recognition as a woman “who keeps the flame of democracy burning amid a growing darkness.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, REGINA GARCIA CANO and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Russian strikes wound at least 20 in Ukraine's capital as child is killed in separate attack
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drone and missile strikes wounded at least 20 people in Kyiv, damaged residential buildings and caused blackouts across swaths of Ukraine early Friday, authorities said. A child also was killed in separate attacks in the southeast of the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican leaders backed Venezuela's Machado for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., signed a letter supporting opposition leader María Corina Machado.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Nobel winner dedicates peace prize to people of Venezuela, Trump
item.description: This year's Nobel Peace Prize winner, Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado, has dedicated her award to the people of her country and to President Trump for his "decisive support” for democracy in Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize chair responds to Trump's pressure campaign
item.description: The chair of the Nobel Peace Prize committee responded Friday to President Trump's public pressure campaign to win the award, telling reporters that the deliberation process for choosing the awardee prioritizes courage and integrity.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel-Hamas ceasefire takes effect, IDF begins pulling back in Gaza
item.description: Israeli troops began repositioning to pre-determined locations as the ceasefire with Hamas began, bringing the hostages one step closer to going home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians are starting to walk north to return to their homes after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that troops were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump doesn’t get Nobel Peace Prize; María Corina Machado awarded
item.description: President Trump failed in his bid to be awarded the Nobel Peace Prize, with the Norwegian Nobel committee announcing Friday that María Corina Machado was given this year’s award for promoting democratic rights for the people of Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president pledges to build air defense system in face of China threat
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan will accelerate the building of a “Taiwan Shield” or “T-Dome” air defense system in the face of the military threat from China, its leader said Friday. President Lai Ching-te also pledged to raise defense spending to more than 3% of GDP and to reach 5% by 2030.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tony Blair is revered in Kosovo for helping end its war. Many ask if he can succeed again in Gaza
item.description: PRISTINA, Kosovo (AP) — A U.S. peace plan has propelled former British Prime Minister Tony Blair to the forefront of efforts to end the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza. While his legacy in the Middle East is controversial, especially given his role in taking the U.K. to war as part of the 2003 U.S.
item.dc:creator: FLORENT BAJRAMI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows to build a 'socialist paradise' ahead of a likely military parade
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un lauded what he called his country's fight against a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Tropical Storm Jerry churns in the Atlantic, Priscilla and a nor'easter threaten US with flooding
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Tropical Storm Jerry churned Thursday in the Atlantic on its approach to the Leeward Islands as Tropical Storm Priscilla and Tropical Storm Raymond moved along Mexico’s Pacific coast, threatening heavy rain and flooding in their paths, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Madagascar's Gen Z protesters clash with police as they call for president to step down
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — About 1,000 protesters marched in Madagascar's capital on Thursday, clashing with police who used tear gas and stun grenades to disperse them in the third week of what has been the most significant unrest in years in the Indian Ocean island nation.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TÉTAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US buys Argentine pesos, finalizes $20 billion currency swap
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States directly purchased Argentine pesos on Thursday and finalized a $20 billion currency swap line with Argentina’s central bank, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a social media post, a rare move aimed at stabilizing turbulent financial markets in the cash-stra
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Obama: We 'should all be encouraged' that end to Israel-Hamas war in sight
item.description: Former President Obama on Thursday praised the announcement of the first phase of a peace deal brokered between Israel and Hamas following two years of war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine's new missiles and drones cause gas shortages in Russia, Zelenskyy says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Strikes on Russian oil facilities by Ukraine’s newly developed long-range missiles and drones are causing significant gas shortages in Russia, while on the battlefield a recent Ukrainian counter-offensive has derailed Russia’s plans to capture parts of the eastern Donetsk region
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US diplomat fired over relationship with woman accused of Chinese Communist Party ties
item.description: The dismissal is believed to be the first of its kind for violating a ban on such relationships that was introduced late last year under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Celebrations in Israel, Gaza at first peace deal announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump says the remaining Israeli hostages in Gaza may be released as soon as Monday.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Manchester synagogue attacker 'pledged allegiance to Islamic State,' police say
item.description: The suspect in the deadly attack on a synagogue in Manchester allegedly pledged allegiance to the Islamic State in a call to emergency dispatchers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas Gaza militias reject terror group, declare support for Trump’s peace plan
item.description: Support for a peace plan grows as negotiators work in Cairo to seal the deal, with Gazan militias backing Trump’s proposal amid fighting and calls for new local governance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe takes on 'hybrid war' as Germany allows police to shoot down drones
item.description: EU President Ursula von der Leyen warns Russia's 'hybrid warfare' targeting Europe through airspace violations and cyber attacks needs to be directly acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strike seriously damages Ukrainian power plant as winter approaches, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia seriously damaged one of Ukraine’s thermal power plants in an overnight attack, authorities said Wednesday, as Moscow pursued its campaign to deny Ukrainians heat, light and running water as winter approaches.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel seizes another Gaza flotilla
item.description: Israel intercepted another flotilla attempting to break the naval blockade and reach Gaza. This comes just days after another fleet was intercepted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Trump’s Gaza deal nears, family warns Israel not to free another Sinwar
item.description: As President Trump pushes to finalize a Gaza ceasefire and hostage exchange, one Israeli family warns that freeing a notorious Hamas terrorist could lead to new bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu, on 2-year mark of Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack, says Israel 'not broken,' vows to bring hostages home
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu marked two years since Hamas’ Oct. 7 assault in a statement issued on X, honoring the victims and vowing Israel’s war for survival continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newly elected German mayor fights for life after stabbing attack
item.description: German mayor-elect Iris Stalzer was hospitalized in critical condition following a stabbing attack at her home in the German town of Herdecke.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says US hit another boat off Venezuela coast on Saturday
item.description: It was not immediately clear if the president was referencing a strike announced Friday by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation
